requirements.description: The contract must consume checked-in provider streaming, RPC output pressure, and TUI degradation evidence sources rather than live telemetry or regenerated cargo artifacts.
Every surface must map to event count, semantic preservation, low-value coalescing, pressure depth, latency or deterministic step evidence, and verdict fields.
If any required surface is absent, fails, lacks semantic-loss assertions, or lacks a canonical metric mapping, the aggregate status must be fail.
The output must distinguish semantic preservation from low-value delta coalescing so operators can interpret provider, RPC, and TUI pressure as one budget.
The contract is read-only evidence aggregation. It does not duplicate pressure tests, enforce runtime throttles, run cargo, regenerate child evidence, or authorize release-facing performance claims.
